sections.paragraph:
κυοφορέω (κύος [=κύημα] ‘embryo, fetus’ + φορέω ‘carry [for an extended time]’, s. also κύω; Hippocr. et al.; M. Ant. 9, 3, 2; Eccl 11:5; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 102 and oft.; Just., D. 33, 4) be pregnant, metaph. be fruitful γῆ κυοφοροῦσα the fruitful earth 1 Cl 20:4 (cp. Philo, Opif. Mundi 43). Pass. be conceived (Artem. 4, 84; Galen XIX p. 174 K.) of Jesus ἐκυοφορήθη ὑπὸ Μαρίας he was conceived by Mary IEph 18:2. τὸν προφητικὸν λόγον ὸ̔ς ἐ[κ]υοφορήθη … ὑπʼ αὐτῆς AcPl Ha 8, 27f=BMM recto 36.—DELG s.v. κυέω.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

metaph. metaph. = metaphor(ically)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 575
